meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop drifting in tutorial hell. Learn how to implement founder-focused analytics to validate your MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start measuring. By integrating lightweight analytics into your MVP, you force yourself to track actual user behavior. This shifts your focus from 'learning to code' to 'validating market demand,' turning your development process into a data-driven path toward sustainable, recurring revenue.
content.intro:
You are trapped in a loop. You’ve watched the tutorials, copied the syntax, and deployed the boilerplate, yet your product feels like a ghost town. Tutorial hell is the silent killer of ambitious founders—it’s the comfort of consumption masquerading as the progress of building. You aren't building a product; you’re building a portfolio of abandoned side projects.

True founders don't finish tutorials; they ship features and iterate based on cold, hard data. By instrumenting your application with analytics from day one, you transform your project from a static codebase into a living, breathing business. It’s time to stop guessing what your users want and start watching them show you.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one action that signals value delivery. For a SaaS, this is usually 'Account Created' or 'First Report Generated'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a schema for a PostHog event tracker that captures user onboarding completion and the specific feature they interact with first.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Inject the tracking client into your app's core entry point. This ensures every route is covered without manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the main application provider to include a global analytics wrapper that initializes on app load.

steps.title: Instrument Key User Flows
steps.description: Wrap your primary call-to-action buttons with event triggers to capture intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable tracking hook that sends event data to the backend whenever a 'Submit' button is pressed.

steps.title: Implement Session Replay
steps.description: Visualizing user friction is more powerful than logs. Enable session recording to see exactly where users get stuck.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the session replay middleware to capture UI interactions while sanitizing PII (Personally Identifiable Information).

steps.title: Automate Weekly Insight Reporting
steps.description: Don't check dashboards daily. Automate a weekly summary to maintain focus on high-impact iterations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that queries the analytics API for active user counts and emails a summary every Monday morning.

pro_tips: Track 'negative' events like 'error-encountered' just as aggressively as 'success' events.
Never over-track; focus on 3 core events that correlate directly with user retention.
Sanitize your data early; GDPR compliance is a feature, not an afterthought.
Use session replays to identify UI bugs that your unit tests will never catch.
If no one is clicking your button, it's not a bug; it's a value proposition failure.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, wait for 10 users to interact with your MVP. Review the event logs, identify the highest drop-off point, and use Claude Code to refactor that specific flow.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start building for growth. You have the tools, the insights, and the roadmap to move from dreamer to founder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
